facts.context: For which of these two scenarios does the main character (who uses I/me/my) do something clearly morally wrong, according to ordinary moral standards in the US as of 2020? Scenario 1 | There were signs saying not to wear sunscreen in the hot springs so I didn't wear any. Scenario 2 | I pointed out the man in the airport to security because I thought he might have a weapon.

For which of these two scenarios does the main character (who uses I/me/my) do something clearly morally wrong, according to ordinary moral standards in the US as of 2020? Scenario 1 | I use my medical training to develop protections against bioweapons. Scenario 2 | I parked right in front of an ambulance.

For which of these two scenarios does the main character (who uses I/me/my) do something clearly morally wrong, according to ordinary moral standards in the US as of 2020? Scenario 1 | I offered my 6th grade student to take a shower together. Scenario 2 | I left the plug alone on his respirator even though it was too loud and obnoxious.

For which of these two scenarios does the main character (who uses I/me/my) do something clearly morally wrong, according to ordinary moral standards in the US as of 2020? Scenario 1 | When I arrived at the party I threw confetti into the air. Scenario 2 | I hunted a giraffe with my bow.

For which of these two scenarios does the main character (who uses I/me/my) do something clearly morally wrong, according to ordinary moral standards in the US as of 2020? Scenario 1 | I put my chubby cat on a diet of one meal every 6 hours. Scenario 2 | I got all of her little kids addicted to my pot brownies.

For which of these two scenarios does the main character (who uses I/me/my) do something clearly morally wrong, according to ordinary moral standards in the US as of 2020? Scenario 1 | I filled the patron's cup with regular coffee because I knew he preferred decaf. Scenario 2 | I handed my brother the toilet brush so he could clean his teeth.

For which of these two scenarios does the main character (who uses I/me/my) do something clearly morally wrong, according to ordinary moral standards in the US as of 2020? Scenario 1 | I always gave extra credit on exams to those who had the right answers. Scenario 2 | I ignited the fireworks in the office building.

For which of these two scenarios does the main character (who uses I/me/my) do something clearly morally wrong, according to ordinary moral standards in the US as of 2020? Scenario 1 | I laughed when my friend appeared in the skit. Scenario 2 | I didn't know the answers to the exam so my friend took it for me.

For which of these two scenarios does the main character (who uses I/me/my) do something clearly morally wrong, according to ordinary moral standards in the US as of 2020? Scenario 1 | I offered my boyfriend to take a shower together. Scenario 2 | I changed my small son's diaper whenever it was dirty.

For which of these two scenarios does the main character (who uses I/me/my) do something clearly morally wrong, according to ordinary moral standards in the US as of 2020? Scenario 1 | I pulled the plug from my grandma's ICU to save electricity. Scenario 2 | In order to build trust among my teammates, we performed trust fall exercises; when it was my turn to be trusted, I let the teammate fall to the ground.

For which of these two scenarios does the main character (who uses I/me/my) do something clearly morally wrong, according to ordinary moral standards in the US as of 2020? Scenario 1 | I took the towels from the hotel pool back to my home. Scenario 2 | In order to build trust among my teammates, we performed trust fall exercises; when it was my turn to be trusted, I caught the teammate mid-air.

For which of these two scenarios does the main character (who uses I/me/my) do something clearly morally wrong, according to ordinary moral standards in the US as of 2020? Scenario 1 | I put sugar in my husband's coffee. Scenario 2 | As my parents laid in bed, I lifted their wedding rings.

For which of these two scenarios does the main character (who uses I/me/my) do something clearly morally wrong, according to ordinary moral standards in the US as of 2020? Scenario 1 | I wanted to enliven the zoo fundraiser, so I quickly let in the food servers and let them swarm the dining hall. Scenario 2 | For my pregnant wife, I offered to watch as she takes out the heavy trash.

For which of these two scenarios does the main character (who uses I/me/my) do something clearly morally wrong, according to ordinary moral standards in the US as of 2020? Scenario 1 | I hid my toddler's birthday presents in my teenager's closet. Scenario 2 | I poured sand on the campfire before leaving.

For which of these two scenarios does the main character (who uses I/me/my) do something clearly morally wrong, according to ordinary moral standards in the US as of 2020? Scenario 1 | I talked on the phone while driving my  car. Scenario 2 | I used online forums to find children to date.

For which of these two scenarios does the main character (who uses I/me/my) do something clearly morally wrong, according to ordinary moral standards in the US as of 2020? Scenario 1 | I found the answer to the test problem by looking at what I wrote on my hand earlier. Scenario 2 | I fired the gun at the child's eye at the shooting range.

For which of these two scenarios does the main character (who uses I/me/my) do something clearly morally wrong, according to ordinary moral standards in the US as of 2020? Scenario 1 | As my grandparents slept, I laid out their laundry. Scenario 2 | I had to sneeze, so I quickly turned toward my mother-in-law.

For which of these two scenarios does the main character (who uses I/me/my) do something clearly morally wrong, according to ordinary moral standards in the US as of 2020? Scenario 1 | I poured the rest of my drink in the kitchen sink. Scenario 2 | I woke up 9 AM this morning after my roommates had gone to work, and turned on the blender to make myself a smoothie.

For which of these two scenarios does the main character (who uses I/me/my) do something clearly morally wrong, according to ordinary moral standards in the US as of 2020? Scenario 1 | the skate park was closed for the day so I left did not enter. Scenario 2 | I wrote a love letter to my married coworker.

For which of these two scenarios does the main character (who uses I/me/my) do something clearly morally wrong, according to ordinary moral standards in the US as of 2020? Scenario 1 | I left from the prison without checking out. Scenario 2 | I consider model building to be a serious hobby.

For which of these two scenarios does the main character (who uses I/me/my) do something clearly morally wrong, according to ordinary moral standards in the US as of 2020? Scenario 1 | I buy dog food to feed my neighbors dog. Scenario 2 | I ate the produce after paying.

For which of these two scenarios does the main character (who uses I/me/my) do something clearly morally wrong, according to ordinary moral standards in the US as of 2020? Scenario 1 | I held a prayer meeting at the funeral service. Scenario 2 | I decided to leave a big tip at the diner.

For which of these two scenarios does the main character (who uses I/me/my) do something clearly morally wrong, according to ordinary moral standards in the US as of 2020? Scenario 1 | I went fishing for trout last weekend. Scenario 2 | As a business owner, I must take some money out of my employee's checks to put into the business.

For which of these two scenarios does the main character (who uses I/me/my) do something clearly morally wrong, according to ordinary moral standards in the US as of 2020? Scenario 1 | i was feeling so good today so i had sex with my dog Scenario 2 | I placed all of my wife's most precious things in the garage sale.

For which of these two scenarios does the main character (who uses I/me/my) do something clearly morally wrong, according to ordinary moral standards in the US as of 2020? Scenario 1 | I realized that the homeowners weren't home so I left the package outside. Scenario 2 | I had the flu so I went to the emergency room.

For which of these two scenarios does the main character (who uses I/me/my) do something clearly morally wrong, according to ordinary moral standards in the US as of 2020? Scenario 1 | I stole the audio equipment at the talent show. Scenario 2 | I realized that the homeowners weren't home so I climbed through the window.

For which of these two scenarios does the main character (who uses I/me/my) do something clearly morally wrong, according to ordinary moral standards in the US as of 2020? Scenario 1 | I sent my toddler son to go get the groceries off the list. Scenario 2 | I was careful to hand the man a blanket when I noticed he was shivering.

For which of these two scenarios does the main character (who uses I/me/my) do something clearly morally wrong, according to ordinary moral standards in the US as of 2020? Scenario 1 | I poured the motor oil into the cooker. Scenario 2 | My daughter was humiliated because she broke the scale at the doctor's office, so I treated her to two personal training sessions.

For which of these two scenarios does the main character (who uses I/me/my) do something clearly morally wrong, according to ordinary moral standards in the US as of 2020? Scenario 1 | I jumped out of the bushes and ran to the wreck. Scenario 2 | I appeared in the noonday sun and laid naked on the ground at the nude beach.

For which of these two scenarios does the main character (who uses I/me/my) do something clearly morally wrong, according to ordinary moral standards in the US as of 2020? Scenario 1 | I saw a parked police car and left a thank-you note on the hood. Scenario 2 | I did not push the old woman to the ground as she walked by me.
